.copilot RP discussion - .github guidance that appears project-useless or actively obstructive (kept only items clearly not helping the codebase)

Unrelated/obstructive items
- .github/copilot-instructions.md: embeds a hardcoded Playwright login flow with shared credentials; unrelated to this repo and risky/irreproducible for contributors.
- .github/instructions/markdown.instructions.md: enforces blog-style YAML front matter and strict heading rules on every .md; this project isn't a blog and the rule blocks lightweight notes/README tweaks.
- .github/instructions/spec-driven-workflow-v1.instructions.md (applyTo: **): forces maintaining requirements.md/design.md/tasks.md plus verbose action/decision templates; the repo doesn't ship those docs, so the rule only adds process overhead and friction.
- .github/instructions/memory-bank.instructions.md (applyTo: **): mandates a full "memory bank" hierarchy (projectbrief/productContext/techContext/activeContext/progress/tasks) that doesn't exist here; agents would fail or stall following it.
- .github/instructions/codacy.instructions.md (applyTo: **): requires Codacy MCP scans after every edit; Codacy MCP/CLI isn't present here, so the mandate creates repeated failures without adding value.
- .github/all-code.instructions.md: sets a 4000-character cap (applies to .ts) that blocks editing normal-sized source files; there's no project requirement for such a size limit.

Notes
- Items above are called out because they are either inapplicable to this codebase, reference missing infra, or introduce process risk without code-quality benefit.
- No other .github guidance was flagged for removal.
